origin: A passage of Koine Greek biblical genealogy, most likely from the Gospel of Matthew (Matt. 1:6), listing David’s lineage—Jesse → David → Solomon—using phrasing characteristic of the Greek scriptural tradition.
author: Anonymous, with the text traditionally attributed to the evangelist Matthew (or a Matthean tradition/editor compiling Matthew). Actual wording here reads like scripture text rather than a personal, newly authored narrative voice.
thoughts: The writer/editor is likely focused on establishing legal/ancestral legitimacy for the Messiah by tracing a Davidic royal line. Mentioning Solomon “from the wife of Uriah” signals a known historical/biblical detail (connected with Bathsheba), showing continuity of God’s purposes even through morally complicated human history.
audience: Jewish or Jewish-Christian readers who would recognize and value genealogical proof and scriptural fulfillment—people interested in demonstrating that Jesus belongs to David’s line.
written_when: Probably in the late 1st century CE, commonly dated to roughly 80–90 CE (with some scholarship allowing somewhat earlier/later ranges).
